health_zone_table.basis: SitRep56 Table 2 (2026-07-09) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP PDF (post 25126, media 25127, pdf sha256 a195bf3c631a8e3547206a5085db54b29861d17e3b1d5f3a291e96160f9f989b, 13 pages, 1491771 bytes), byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt (all 13 pages inspected clean).

reconciliation.note: Named health-zone rows sum to 1813 confirmed / 648 confirmed deaths. Table 1 province totals are Ituri 1663/553, Nord-Kivu 164/94, Sud-Kivu 3/1 = national 1830/648. The Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified is retained as the explicit unventilated row. Unlike SitRep 55, Nord-Kivu's Table 1 total (164/94) equals the sum of its Table 2 named zone rows this cycle, so there is NO Nord-Kivu ventilation gap; the derived unallocated residual is 17 confirmed / 0 deaths (Ituri only). Sud-Kivu recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26 and, as of 2026-07-07, has passed 42 consecutive days (twice the maximum incubation period) with no new confirmed case, opening the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 104 analyzed / 32 positive (30.8%); Nord-Kivu 108 analyzed / 6 positive (5.6%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 38 positives over 212 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep56 Tableau 7: 780 in isolation at end of day (238 confirmed / 542 suspected) at 100.3% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu at 160.0% saturation, Ituri 89.9%, Sud-Kivu 42.2%. Exits over 24h (62 total): 10 deaths (all Ituri), 41 non-cases, 5 recoveries (Ituri 4, NK 1), 4 escapes (all Ituri), 2 transfers to HGR; 89 admissions across the three provinces.
Recovered advanced to 300 DRC cumulative (+5). J-1 bed count is 755; the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 780 (238 + 542).
highlights: 38 new confirmed cases and 23 deaths (73.9% community) were reported for 2026-07-09, in Ituri (32 cases, 18 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (6 cases, 5 deaths). Confirmed cases rose to 1830 and deaths to 648; the global CFR is 35.4%. The headline is flagged 'Donnees reactualisees' (DHIS2 base cleaning/harmonization).

No newly affected health zone this cycle; affected zones hold at 37/104. The evolving situation in Tshopo and Haut-Uele was discussed at ministerial level; those provinces remain outside the three officially affected provinces and are EXCLUDED from totals.
The provider strike continues in Bunia and Rwampara (Ituri). Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 160% and global bed occupancy is 100.3% (780 in isolation, 238 confirmed / 542 suspected).

Ituri concentrates 90.1% of cases (n=1663) and 85.3% of deaths (n=553), CFR 33.3%. Bunia (495), Rwampara (373), Mongbwalu (327), Nizi (107) and Nyankunde (97) remain the most affected zones; the epidemic is currently progressing fastest in Nizi.

Nord-Kivu totals 164 confirmed / 94 deaths, CFR 57.3%, carried by Katwa (68.2%), Beni (66.7%) and Butembo (46.5%); Katwa (66), Butembo (43) and Beni (30) lead notification. Vuhovi shows a 100% CFR on a very small count.

Sud-Kivu has passed 42 consecutive days without a new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26; milestone reached 2026-07-07), opening the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration; 19 suspects remain in isolation and 2 samples are under analysis.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 3 (09 July): Report (J-1) 119; new alerts 1232; total alerts of the day 1351. Investigated 1069 (79.1%). Validated suspects 284 = 203 living + 81 deaths, concordant with the banner suspected-cases-of-day 284 (of which 81 deaths under investigation for possible reclassification).

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 160.0%; national isolation occupancy is 100.3% (780 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 78.1% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert investigation in Ituri (62.1% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers (112 HCW cases, 35 deaths, CFR 31.2%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.3% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two Kisangani (Tshopo) detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) are under investigation and 159912 travelers passed through PoE/PoC this cycle.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu reports a laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis (5.6% positivity) and, in its logistics subset, no functional ambulance or hearse; Mutwanga and Kasindi-Lubiriha flag insufficient IPC inputs and swabs for community-death alerts.
challenges.response: Clear the laboratory backlog, restore transport/EDS logistics, and resupply IPC inputs and swabs to the affected Nord-Kivu health zones.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (09 July): 12946 under follow-up, 10106 seen, 78.1%. Ituri 75.7% (10744/8136), Nord-Kivu 89.5% (2202/1970). Sud-Kivu contact follow-up closed (all listed contacts completed). Tableau 5: 645 new contacts listed (Ituri 524, Nord-Kivu 121), 6 became suspect (Ituri 4, Nord-Kivu 2), 163 completed 21-day follow-up (Ituri 92, Nord-Kivu 71).

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep56 Tableau 7 end-of-day census 780 at 100.3% occupancy (Ituri 536 @89.9%, Nord-Kivu 225 @160.0%, Sud-Kivu 19 @42.2%). Census 780 = 238 confirmed + 542 suspected; province rows 536+225+19=780 concordant. J-1 bed count 755. Exits (62 total): 10 deaths (all Ituri), 41 non-cases (Ituri 27, NK 6, SK 8), 5 recoveries (Ituri 4, NK 1), 4 escapes (all Ituri), 2 transfers to HGR (Ituri); 89 admissions (Ituri 43, NK 44, SK 2).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones, and validate the Kisangani/Tshopo and Haut-Uele detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) before any count-bearing use.

Relieve isolation load at 100.3% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 160% saturation (57.3% provincial CFR); close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction/expansion (Bunia ETC expanding 60 to 80 beds).

Improve contact follow-up (78.1% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 62.1%, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog / delayed-diagnosis and the reported lack of a functional ambulance/hearse.

Sustain residual epidemiological surveillance in Sud-Kivu (42+ days with no new confirmed case as of 2026-07-07) toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration, while resolving the 19 suspects still in isolation and 2 samples under analysis.

preserved_conflicts: SitRep56 headline is DRC-only (1830 confirmed / 648 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.

Nord-Kivu Table 1 total (164/94) EQUALS the sum of its Table 2 named zone rows this cycle, so unlike SitRep 55 there is no Nord-Kivu ventilation gap. The only unallocated residual is the 17 Ituri confirmed cases in zones not yet identified (0 deaths), retained as an explicit unventilated row and never smeared across named zones.

Suspected cases of the day total 284 = 203 living + 81 deaths (Tableau 3), concordant with the banner. The 81 community deaths are under investigation for possible reclassification (Non-cas / cas probables).

Sud-Kivu passed 42 consecutive days with no new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26; milestone 2026-07-07). It remains one of the three officially affected provinces pending an end-of-outbreak declaration; 19 suspects are still in isolation and 2 samples are under analysis.

Detections in Kisangani/Tshopo and Haut-Uele were discussed at ministerial level but the three officially affected provinces are unchanged, so these signals are EXCLUDED from totals and carried on the suspected-watch overlay only.

review_notes: SitRep #056 (report date 2026-07-09; INSP post 25126; media 25127; WP publish 2026-07-11T11:38:14Z). PDF sha256 a195bf3c631a8e3547206a5085db54b29861d17e3b1d5f3a291e96160f9f989b, 13 pages, 1491771 bytes, byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt (all 13 pages rendered and inspected clean).

DRC 1830 confirmed / 648 deaths / CFR 35.4% / 300 recovered; +38 confirmed / +23 deaths in 24h (Ituri 32/18, Nord-Kivu 6/5, Sud-Kivu 0). 37/104 affected zones, no new zone. Isolation census 780 = 238 confirmed + 542 suspected at 100.3% occupancy. Contact follow-up 78.1%.

Nord-Kivu Table 1 == Table 2 named sum this cycle (164/94), so the only unallocated residual is the 17 Ituri unventilated cases; country-scope unallocated is 37 (17 Ituri + 20 Uganda). Sud-Kivu passed 42 days with no new confirmed case.
